proposals.title: Federal constitutional amendment
proposals.example: American Promise — For Our Freedom Amendment
proposals.advantages: Could directly address corporate personhood and spending doctrine if ratified
proposals.challenges: High bar for ratification; long timeline; political coordination across states

proposals.title: Federal campaign finance legislation
proposals.example: Disclosure, coordination, or public financing bills
proposals.advantages: National scope; can address disclosure and coordination within constitutional limits
proposals.challenges: Citizens United constrains independent expenditure limits; partisan gridlock

proposals.title: FEC structure and enforcement reforms
proposals.advantages: Could strengthen enforcement of existing rules
proposals.challenges: Commission deadlock; scope limited by court precedent

proposals.title: Enhanced disclosure laws
proposals.advantages: Transparency without direct spending limits; broad public support in principle
proposals.challenges: Dark money routing; constitutional limits on disclosure of minor donors

proposals.title: Public campaign financing
proposals.advantages: Reduces candidate dependence on large donors when voluntary
proposals.challenges: Funding source debates; voluntary vs. mandatory constitutional questions

proposals.title: Shareholder / member consent requirements
proposals.advantages: Corporate governance path without constitutional amendment
proposals.challenges: Limited to corporate structure; enforcement and scope questions

proposals.title: State ballot initiatives
proposals.advantages: Direct democracy where permitted; state-level experimentation
proposals.challenges: Preemption and constitutional challenges; not available in all states

proposals.title: State corporate charter theory
proposals.advantages: Uses state corporate law rather than direct expenditure bans
proposals.challenges: First Amendment litigation expected; Citizens United precedent

proposals.title: Arkansas General Assembly — state policy options
proposals.advantages: State-specific disclosure, ethics, or education statutes
proposals.challenges: Arkansas is not a citizen initiative state; legislative path only

proposals.title: Anti-coordination rules
proposals.challenges: Proof and definition difficulties; enforcement gaps

proposals.title: Litigation strategy
proposals.advantages: Tests boundaries of Citizens United and state authority
proposals.status_detail: Maine Super PAC limits litigation; Hawaii Act 11 expected challenge

proposals.title: Community education and civic forums
proposals.advantages: No legislation required; builds informed electorate
proposals.challenges: Scale and sustainability depend on volunteer leadership
